@@ -0,0 +1,207 @@
+# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-249
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-28T16:36:47+03:00
+RELATED_FILES:
+- crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs — split `init_render_state`
+  into Phase 1 (unconditional window/gfx/surface/egui/inspector) and
+  Phase 2 (cuboid pipeline gated on `has_cad_scene ||
+  has_prebuilt_mesh`); added egui-only `render_frame_egui_only` branch
+  invoked by `render_frame` whenever Phase 2 did not populate the
+  cuboid pipeline / frame graph.
+STATUS: AWAITING_REVIEW
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-249_TASK_2026-05-28_16-13-25+0300.md`
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs`:
+  - **`init_render_state` split into two phases.** Phase 1 runs
+    unconditionally on production `resumed`: constructs the
+    `winit::Window`, `GfxContext`, `SurfaceContext`, then (after the
+    Phase 2 branch) constructs `EguiHost` + clones the
+    `Arc<InspectorHandoff>`. Phase 2 is gated on `has_cad_scene ||
+    has_prebuilt_mesh` and delegates to the unchanged
+    `init_render_state_post_surface` helper (so the helper's mesh-side
+    assertions remain intact and only run when meshes are actually
+    available). In the empty-world branch the freshly-constructed
+    `GfxContext` is stashed into `self.gfx_ctx` so the unconditional
+    egui-host construction block (which reads `self.gfx_ctx.as_ref()`)
+    succeeds. Updated the method's docstring to describe the two-phase
+    contract.
+  - **Phase 2 guard preserved.** The cuboid pipeline + render-mesh
+    upload + frame-graph substrate + per-mesh material build still run
+    only inside `init_render_state_post_surface`, which is now called
+    only inside the `has_cad_scene || has_prebuilt_mesh` branch. The
+    helper's contract is unchanged; the mesh-side debug_assert pair
+    (`!materials.is_empty()`, `uploaded_meshes.len() == materials.len()`)
+    is still reached exactly when a mesh source exists.
+  - **`render_frame` early-dispatch.** A new guard at the top of
+    `render_frame` checks `self.pipeline.is_none() ||
+    self.compiled_frame_graph.is_none()`. Either being absent means
+    Phase 2 did not run (both fields are stashed together at the end
+    of `init_render_state_post_surface`, so partial state is not
+    possible). When that branch fires, `render_frame` returns the
+    result of `render_frame_egui_only` directly — the cuboid /
+    frame-graph / depth-view code path is never entered.
+  - **New private helper `render_frame_egui_only`.** Acquires the
+    surface texture (with the same skip-on-transient policy as the
+    cuboid path), issues a clear-only render pass to `DEFAULT_CLEAR`,
+    publishes the inspector handoff via `self.inspector_snapshot()`,
+    runs `EguiHost::render` against the same encoder, submits, presents,
+    and returns `true`. Returns `false` when any of `surface_ctx` /
+    `window` / `gfx_ctx` / `egui_host` is `None` so the
+    "`EguiHost`-exists ⇒ true" contract from the acceptance criteria
+    holds. Never references the cuboid pipeline, frame graph, depth
+    view, or `encode_main_pass`.
+
+### Tests
+
+- No new tests added. The lifecycle-test decision tree fell into the
+  third outcome: no direct test of `init_render_state` exists in
+  `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs` (a `Grep` for
+  `init_render_state` over that file returned only three matches, all
+  in doc-comments referring to the helper's contract), and production
+  `init_render_state` requires winit's runtime-only `ActiveEventLoop`
+  parameter, which cannot be constructed without spinning a real winit
+  event loop. Per the Task Packet's explicit guidance for that
+  outcome, no fake-runtime test was added.
+- All 146 pre-existing `rge-editor-shell` lib tests still pass; the
+  test-count delta is **+0** (146 → 146). The 1 ignored test is the
+  pre-existing release-only `render_frame_e2e_perf` harness.
+
+### Docs
+
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs`: updated the `init_render_state`
+  docstring to describe the Phase 1 / Phase 2 split and updated the
+  `render_frame` docstring to document the egui-only dispatch.
+  `render_frame_egui_only` is documented inline at its definition. No
+  other doc files (Status.md, HANDOFF.md, change.md, plans/**) were
+  touched — those snapshots are outside this dispatch's edit envelope.
+
+## Per-File Summary
+
+- **`crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs`** is the only modified
+  tracked file. `git diff --stat` reports 200 insertions / 30 deletions
+  on this file alone.
+- **`crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs`** was NOT touched. The
+  task allowed editing only if a real lifecycle tolerance hole existed
+  for the Phase 1-only state. The audit traced through every
+  lifecycle-side consumer of cuboid render fields:
+  - `ApplicationHandler::resumed` propagates the `init_render_state`
+    error and never inspects pipeline-side state directly.
+  - `window_event::WindowEvent::Resized` calls `resize_render_path`,
+    which already gates the camera UBO update on
+    `self.gfx_camera.as_ref()` and the frame-graph rebuild on
+    `self.compiled_frame_graph.is_some()`; both are `None` in Phase
+    1-only state and the existing `if let` / `if` checks make those
+    branches no-ops. The surface resize itself uses only `gfx_ctx` +
+    `surface_ctx`, both of which are populated by Phase 1.
+  - `window_event::WindowEvent::RedrawRequested` calls
+    `self.tick_redraw()` (no render-state assumptions) then
+    `self.render_frame()` (now dispatches to the egui-only path
+    cleanly).
+  - `window_event::WindowEvent::MouseInput` calls `handle_left_click`,
+    which is guarded on `projection.is_some()`; world-only shells have
+    `projection == None`, so face-pick stays a no-op — pre-existing
+    contract, not a new hole.
+  - `handle_asset_reload` short-circuits when `glb_source_path ==
+    None`, which is the case for `--scene` launches.
+  No new tolerance was required; the file was therefore left
+  byte-identical, as the task scope explicitly permitted.
+
+## Verification Results
+
+- `git status --short --branch` (pre-edit) → branch
+  `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-249`, tracked state clean; only untracked items
+  were the TASK packet + sidecar created by the orchestrator.
+- `cargo +nightly fmt -p rge-editor-shell -- --check` → exit 0 (after
+  one rustfmt-driven line-wrap correction on the new encoder
+  binding).
+- `cargo build -p rge-editor-shell -p rge-editor` → exit 0; both
+  crates compile cleanly.
+- `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --lib --no-fail-fast` → **146
+  passed / 0 failed / 1 ignored** (`render_frame_e2e_perf` —
+  pre-existing release-only). Test-count delta vs. pre-dispatch: **+0**.
+- `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` → exit 0; all
+  **9 enforcement PASS** (`forbidden-dep`, `split-exemption`,
+  `no-utils`, `graph-foundation`, `editor-state-ownership`,
+  `command-bus`, `projection-modules`, `kernel-isolation`,
+  `failure-class`, `snapshot-participate`) plus **1 supplementary
+  PASS** (`snapshot-participate` supplementary readout: 3 stateful
+  Tier-2 crates checked, 3 impl SnapshotParticipate, 0 missing). No
+  exemption count shift.
+- `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` → exit 0;
+  `VERIFY OK: all 7 verification step(s) passed.`
+- `git diff --check` → exit 0 (no whitespace errors).
+- `git status --short --branch` (final) → only `M
+  crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs` plus the orchestrator-created
+  untracked `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-249_TASK_*` pair and this EXEC report.
+  Tracked changes are confined to the `MAY edit` envelope.
+
+`cargo test --workspace` was not run — the Task Packet expressly
+prohibits it without a documented reason, and this dispatch's changes
+are bounded internal to `crates/editor-shell`. The dispatch.verify
+substrate ran the full workspace doc-test gate plus the script-bench
+build as part of its 7-step sweep.
+
+## Deviations from Task Packet
+
+None — execution stayed strictly within the Task Packet scope.
+
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs` was the only tracked file
+  edited; it is on the MAY-edit list.
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs` was NOT edited (no
+  lifecycle tolerance hole found, as documented above).
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs` was NOT edited per the
+  third decision-tree outcome (no direct `init_render_state` test
+  exists, and `init_render_state` is not unit-testable without a real
+  `ActiveEventLoop`).
+- No `crates/editor-egui-host/**` file was touched; `EguiHost::new`
+  and `EguiHost::render` are called with their existing signatures.
+- No CLI / `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs` change; scene-load flow
+  through `EditorShell::with_world` is untouched.
+- No new workspace dependencies, no version bumps for egui / wgpu /
+  winit / egui_dock / egui-winit / egui-wgpu.
+
+## Open Questions for Reviewer
+
+None. The two-phase split + egui-only `render_frame` branch are the
+minimum surface required to satisfy the acceptance criteria. The
+empty-world `init_render_state` branch in
+`init_render_state_headless` was left intact: that helper is feature
+/ cfg-gated to test + `test-harness` configurations and is used only
+by the visual-smoke / perf harnesses that explicitly need a mesh
+attached, so its early-return guard remains appropriate. Production
+`init_render_state` is the only path the `--scene` user-visible gap
+flows through, and it is now Phase-1 unconditional.
+
+## Worktree State
+
+- Tracked files modified: `crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs`.
+- Untracked items: `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-249_TASK_2026-05-28_16-13-25+0300.md`,
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-249_TASK_2026-05-28_16-13-25+0300.meta.json`
+  (orchestrator-created), plus this EXEC packet and its sidecar (to
+  be finalized after writing).
+- Branch: `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-249`.
+- Last commit: `e90bdbb` Merge pull request #248 from
+  RustCADs/ai-dispatch/ISSUE-247.
+
+---
+
+HANDOFF_STATUS: COMPLETE
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-249
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+NEXT_ROLE: REVIEWER_AI
+EXIT_CODE: 0
+

@@ -0,0 +1,259 @@
+# Task Packet
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-249
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-28T16:13:25+03:00
+RELATED_FILES:
+- crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs
+- crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs
+- crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs
+- crates/editor-egui-host/src/lib.rs
+- editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-247_EXEC_2026-05-28_14-53-12+0300.md
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-249_*
+- ai_dispatch_logs/log_*ISSUE-249*.md
+STATUS: OPEN
+
+## Goal
+
+Close the user-visible `rge-editor --scene <path>` gap surfaced by ISSUE-247's
+audit Q4/Q5. Today, `init_render_state` in
+`crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs` returns before constructing a winit
+window, `SurfaceContext`, `EguiHost`, or `InspectorHandoff` whenever neither a
+CAD scene nor a prebuilt render mesh is attached. A world-only scene can be
+loaded into `EditorShell`, but it produces no visible editor window. Fix this
+by constructing the production window/surface/egui host for world-only launches
+and by adding an egui-only frame path that can present the dock and Inspector
+chrome without a cuboid render pipeline.
+
+This is a render-init and render-frame ordering change only. The
+`editor-egui-host` crate API stays frozen.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs`
+  - Split `init_render_state` into two phases.
+  - Phase 1 runs unconditionally when production winit `resumed` invokes
+    `init_render_state`: construct the winit window, `GfxContext`,
+    `SurfaceContext`, `EguiHost`, and `InspectorHandoff`.
+  - Phase 2 remains gated by `has_cad_scene || has_prebuilt_mesh`: cuboid
+    pipeline setup and render-mesh upload, including the current mesh-side
+    assertions near `init_render_state_post_surface`.
+  - Add an egui-only branch in `render_frame` that activates when Phase 2 did
+    not construct the cuboid pipeline/frame graph. That branch must acquire the
+    surface texture, clear the target, publish the inspector handoff, call
+    `EguiHost::render`, submit, present, and return `true`.
+  - Preserve the existing cuboid path as the active branch whenever Phase 2 ran.
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs`
+  - Edit only if the new world-only `render_frame` path exposes a real
+    lifecycle tolerance hole, such as a branch that assumes cuboid pipeline
+    state exists after Phase 1-only initialization. If touched, the EXEC report
+    must state the exact hole and why this file was required.
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs`
+  - First locate any existing direct test of `init_render_state` behavior.
+  - If a direct test exists, update its expected behavior to the new contract:
+    empty-world production init creates window/gfx/surface/egui/inspector state
+    while leaving cuboid pipeline state uninitialized.
+  - If no direct test exists and `init_render_state` is unit-testable, add the
+    narrowest feasible test for the new contract.
+  - If no direct test exists and `init_render_state` is not unit-testable
+    because it requires winit's runtime-only `ActiveEventLoop`, do not add a
+    fake-runtime test. Document that outcome explicitly in the EXEC report.
+
+### MUST NOT edit
+
+- `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs`
+- `editor/rge-editor/Cargo.toml`
+- `Cargo.toml`
+- `Cargo.lock`
+- `crates/editor-egui-host/**`
+- `crates/rge-scene-loader/**`
+- `crates/rge-data/**`
+- `crates/editor-ui/**`
+- `crates/editor-actions/**`
+- `crates/editor-state/**`
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/render_frame_e2e_perf.rs`
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/visual_test_harness.rs`
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/visual_smoke.rs`
+- Any other `crates/**`, `kernel/**`, `runtime/**`, or `tools/**` path not
+  explicitly listed under `MAY edit`.
+- `plans/BASELINE.md`
+- `plans/IMPLEMENTATION.md`
+- `plans/PLAN.md`
+- `plans/fileandfolderstructure.md`
+- `plans/README.md`
+- `Status.md`
+- `HANDOFF.md`

+- `README.md`

+- `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`
+- `.ai/*.schema.json`
+- `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`
+- `Invoke-AiDispatch*.ps1`
+- `Register-AiDispatchSchedule.ps1`
+- `Get-AiDispatch*.ps1`
+- `Watch-AiDispatch.ps1`
+- `Wait-GitHubActions.ps1`
+- `new-handoff.ps1`
+- `ai_handoffs/AI_HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md`
+- `ai_handoffs/templates/**`
+- Any prior `ai_handoffs/*` packet or sidecar.
+- Any prior `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md`.
+
+### MAY add new files
+
+- `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-249_*.md`
+- `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-249_*.meta.json`
+- `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*ISSUE-249*.md`
+
+### MUST NOT add new files
+
+- No new Rust source files outside optional edits in the existing
+  `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/tests.rs`.
+- No new crates, Cargo manifests, ADRs, architecture-lint files, automation
+  scripts, schemas, workflow files, or plan docs.
+- No new `.github/` files.
+
+## Deliverables
+
+- `init_render_state` split so Phase 1 always constructs production
+  window/gfx/surface/egui/inspector state, while Phase 2 remains guarded by
+  `has_cad_scene || has_prebuilt_mesh`.
+- `render_frame` extended with an egui-only path for Phase 1-only state:
+  surface acquire, target clear, inspector handoff publish, `EguiHost::render`,
+  submit, present, and `true`.
+- Existing cuboid/default and `--glb` behavior preserved by keeping the cuboid
+  render branch active whenever the cuboid pipeline/frame graph exists.
+- `lifecycle/tests.rs` handled according to the three-outcome decision tree in
+  the Scope section: direct test updated, narrow feasible test added, or
+  runtime-only `ActiveEventLoop` limitation documented.
+- The EXEC report enumerates per-file diffs, `rge-editor-shell` lib test-count
+  delta, which lifecycle-test outcome was taken, whether `lifecycle/mod.rs` was
+  touched and why, and all verification-gate outcomes.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- `rge-editor --scene <path>` now creates a visible production winit window and
+  paints the egui dock plus Inspector tab chrome for a world-only scene.
+- Default-cuboid and `--glb` user-visible output remains unchanged.
+- The world-only `render_frame` path returns `true` when `EguiHost` exists and
+  presents an egui-only frame.
+- The world-only `render_frame` path does not call into cuboid pipeline,
+  frame-graph, or depth-view rendering.
+- `editor-egui-host` public API and files remain byte-for-byte unchanged.
+- W03-style "no scene attached" behavior is handled per the lifecycle-test
+  decision tree, and no existing direct `init_render_state` test is deleted or
+  repurposed.
+- `cargo +nightly fmt -p rge-editor-shell -- --check` exits 0.
+- `cargo build -p rge-editor-shell -p rge-editor` exits 0.
+- `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --lib --no-fail-fast` passes.
+- `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` exits 0 with the
+  expected 9 enforcement PASS results plus 1 supplementary PASS, and no
+  exemption count shift.
+- `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` exits 0.
+- `git diff --check` exits 0.
+- Final tracked changes are confined to the `MAY edit` envelope, with only
+  ISSUE-249 handoff/log artifacts added as allowed.
+
+## Constraints / Non-Goals
+
+- Not a UI redesign. Dock layout, tab bodies, viewport pointer routing, and
+  inspector handoff shape are consumed unchanged.
+- Not a CLI change. `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs` argument parsing, scene
+  load path, and dispatch logic stay untouched.
+- No new scene-loading logic. `--scene` continues to flow through
+  `EditorShell::with_world`.
+- No changes to `crates/editor-egui-host/**`. If this task requires changing
+  `EguiHost` construction, `EguiHost::render`, or any public host API, halt.
+- No edits to perf or visual-smoke harnesses; they use
+  `init_render_state_headless`, not production `init_render_state`.
+- No `cargo test --workspace` unless the Executor documents a specific reason.
+- No new workspace dependencies and no version bumps for egui, egui-winit,
+  egui-wgpu, egui_dock, wgpu, or winit.
+
+## Verification Gates
+
+The Executor MUST run and document the result of each of these in their
+`EXECUTION_REPORT`:
+
+- `git status --short --branch` before any edit; expected clean tracked state
+  on the dispatch branch.
+- `cargo +nightly fmt -p rge-editor-shell -- --check`; expected exit 0.
+- `cargo build -p rge-editor-shell -p rge-editor`; expected exit 0.
+- `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --lib --no-fail-fast`; expected all tests
+  pass, including any updated or added lifecycle test.
+- `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all`; expected exit 0 with
+  9 enforcement PASS results and 1 supplementary PASS, and no exemption count
+  shift.
+- `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`; expected exit 0. Report the verification log's
+  `VERIFY OK: all N verification step(s) passed` summary line.
+- `git diff --check`; expected exit 0.
+- `git status --short --branch` final; expected tracked changes confined to
+  the `MAY edit` envelope and only allowed ISSUE-249 handoff/log artifacts
+  added.
+
+Do not run `cargo test --workspace` unless a specific reason is documented.
+This is a bounded internal-to-shell dispatch.
+
+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt with `HANDOFF_STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`,
+`NEXT_ROLE: HUMAN_ARBITER`, and `EXIT_CODE: 2` if any of the following occur:
+
+- Tracked files are dirty before any edit is attempted.
+- Splitting `init_render_state` requires modifying any
+  `crates/editor-egui-host/**` file or changing the `EguiHost` constructor or
+  public API.
+- Adding the egui-only `render_frame` path requires modifying any
+  `crates/editor-egui-host/**` file or changing the `EguiHost::render`
+  signature or contract.
+- `init_render_state_post_surface` mesh-side assertions cannot be cleanly
+  deferred behind the `has_cad_scene || has_prebuilt_mesh` guard without
+  rewriting that helper's contract.
+- W03-style behavior handling would require deleting an existing direct
+  `init_render_state` test instead of updating it or adding narrower coverage.
+- More than one follow-up dispatch would be required to make the new behavior
+  shippable.
+- Any required verification gate fails for reasons inside this dispatch's
+  changed surface and cannot be fixed within the `MAY edit` envelope.
+- The final diff touches any `MUST NOT edit` path, adds any forbidden file, or
+  shifts an architecture-lint exemption count.
+
+## Planner Notes
+
+This dispatch is grounded in ISSUE-247's audit packet
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-247_EXEC_2026-05-28_14-53-12+0300.md`, specifically the Q5
+bounded-chip recommendation in EXEC round 1. The Q4 finding established that
+`--scene` currently produces no visible window because `init_render_state`
+returns before window/surface/egui-host construction when neither a CAD scene
+nor a prebuilt mesh is present. Subsequent review also identified that
+`render_frame` begins with the depth-view/frame-graph path, so a Phase 1-only
+world path would still need an egui-only presenter to paint.
+
+Intended publication path: queue PR mode
+(`Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1 -PublishMode pr`). The dispatch branch is pushed
+and a PR is opened for human review without merging to `origin/main` and without
+closing the GitHub issue automatically.
+
+Per orchestrator dispatch semantics, TASK and EXEC packets are expected, plus
+an optional CORRECT packet only if a correction round runs. Missing `_REVIEW_`
+or `_CLOSEOUT_` packets is normal for an orchestrator dispatch; Codex control
+review output is written to the gitignored `.ai/dispatch-ISSUE-249/` run
+directory rather than to a packet.
+
+Forward-only doc snapshots in `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, and `change.md` are
+not in this dispatch's edit envelope. Any documentation reconciliation should be
+a separate hygiene dispatch if the execution reveals one is needed.
+
